top_signals.title: AI Agents and the Unseen Work of War
top_signals.summary:
Armies run on more than what happens at the front. Behind every operation is a vast amount of coordination, administration, logistics, and judgment. Bill Pessin, senior vice president of national security at Salesforce and a former U.S. Army logistics officer, joins Jonathan to discuss how military organizations can use AI agents, what makes these tools different from ordinary software, and why safety and accountability matter when new technology enters national security work. They also discuss what Pessin learned early in his Army career about the gap between plans, systems, and the people who have to make them work. Image: Petty

The post AI Agents and the Unseen Work of War appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Chinese Copper Supplier Says US Demand Can Bear Trump’s Tariffs
top_signals.summary: Chinese copper manufacturer Zhejiang Hailiang Co. is betting its American customers won’t balk at higher prices if the US follows though on placing tariffs on the refined metal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

Boris Vujčić: Outlook for the euro area economy and monetary policy
South Korea Delays Single-Stock Weekly Options Days Before Start
top_signals.summary: South Korea has delayed a plan to launch weekly single-stock options on shares including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., the latest sign regulators are becoming jittery after wild stock-market swings.

top_signals.title: Putin’s system is in a state of slow implosion
top_signals.summary: Look to the erosion of fiscal discipline to see where the problems lie
